headlines.title: Jill Biden says she thought Joe Biden was ‘having a stroke’ during 2024 debate
headlines.description: Former first lady Jill Biden said in an interview clip released Wednesday that she thought her husband, former President Joe Biden, was “having a stroke” during his critical June 2024 debate, which catalyzed the chain of events that eventually led him to drop out of the presidential election.“I don’t know what happened.

headlines.title: Republicans brace for money problems in Texas after Ken Paxton’s win
headlines.description: Ken Paxton has trailed Democratic state Rep. James Talarico in fundraising. Some Republicans say the party will have to spend millions to win the Senate race.

headlines.title: Iran and U.S. exchange new strikes as Trump dismisses pressure to end war
headlines.description: Iran fired a missile at an American airbase in Kuwait Thursday just hours after the U.S. attacked the Islamic Republic, with both sides accusing the other of violating their shaky ceasefire and further imperiling diplomatic efforts to end the war

headlines.title: Jill Biden Says She Was ‘Terrified’ Seeing Joe Biden’s 2024 Debate
headlines.description: In a new interview, former first lady Jill Biden made the stunning admission that she was frightened by her husband Joe Biden’s debate performance in June of 2024 that ultimately ended his campaign. “I don’t know what happened. I thought, ‘Oh my God he’s having a stroke,’ and it scared me to death,” she reveals. NBC’s Hallie Jackson reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump Says He Feels No Political Pressure to End War With Iran
headlines.description: As strikes strain the ceasefire between the United States and Iran, at home, President Donald Trump held a cabinet meeting where he defended the war and insisted he’s not concerned with the war’s political consequences heading into the critical midterm elections. It comes as the president is threatening to "blow up" ally Oman in response to a deal in which Iran and Oman would share control of the Strait of Hormuz. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: US-Iran Peace Talks in Doubt After Second Clash in 3 Days
headlines.description: The United States and Iran once again exchanged fire, sending oil prices rising and casting new doubt on peace talks. Iranian media report that Iran's forces first fired warning shots as American ships tried to pass through the Strait of Hormuz without authorization and that the U.S. military responded with strikes. An American official described the strikes as self-defense. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Vance calls Pope Leo’s AI warnings ‘profound’
headlines.description: Vance said in an interview with NBC News he is glad the pope tackled AI. He also discussed why X remains off his phone after he deleted it for Lent.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Today: Laura Jarrett answers your questions about the Supreme Court and more
headlines.description: Today is a decision day at the Supreme Court, which has yet to issue rulings on consequential cases like Trump v. Barbara on birthright citizenship or Trump v. Lisa Cook, the Federal Reserve official.

headlines.title: Trump’s May primary scorecard: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, we take stock of President Donald Trump’s retribution campaign this month as Ken Paxton and James Talarico look ahead to the general election in Texas.

headlines.title: Proposed bill would tax New Yorkers who tap ‘anti-weaponization’ fund at 100%
headlines.description: A New York lawmaker says state residents who tap the Trump administration’s controversial $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund should be required to pay a 100% state income tax on the money.
